title: Private memory overlay: HUNCH_PRIVATE_DIR second store, unioned at read, leak-safe
context: The hunch repo is PUBLIC, so the committed .hunch/ graph (decisions/bugs/constraints) and docs/ are all public. The maintainer needs sensitive engineering memory + strategy docs + secrets kept in a SEPARATE private repo that Hunch can still query/use during local dev.
decision: Add an optional private overlay store. When HUNCH_PRIVATE_DIR (env) points at a hunch-layout dir (a separate private repo the user controls), HunchStore constructs a second JsonStore over it. A new loadAllMerged(kind) returns public ∪ private records (deduped by id, private wins). The READ/QUERY/REINDEX paths use loadAllMerged so MCP tools + the guards see private memory; the local derived SQLite (gitignored) is populated from the merged set. CRITICAL leak-safety: every PUBLIC-artifact writer keeps using the public-only loadAll — updateClaudeMd counts/grounding, replaceAll, and default put() all stay public-only, so a private record can NEVER be written into a committed public file. Writes route to private explicitly via a --private CLI flag / private:true on hunch_record_decision. Env-based (not config.json) so no path is committed and it's machine-specific.
consequences: Hunch becomes usable for OSS-with-private-context: public repo ships a public graph; sensitive memory lives in a private repo unioned only locally.
Leakage is the kill-risk — the public/merged split must be exact; a single public writer reading merged data would leak. Must be covered by explicit no-leak tests + an adversarial review pass.

Phase 2 stands up the private repo + migrates the chosen sensitive content; Phase 3 (DESTRUCTIVE, separately confirmed) scrubs already-public content via history rewrite + force-push — which reduces future exposure but does NOT un-publish already-cloned/cached copies.
Secrets that were ever public must be ROTATED, not just scrubbed (none are currently committed; the npm token was only ever in chat).
alternatives_rejected: Make loadAll() itself union globally — rejected: every public writer (grounding, replaceAll, compact) would then leak private records into committed files; the explicit loadAllMerged at read sites is leak-safe by default.
Store the private path in committed .hunch/config.json — rejected for v1: commits a machine-specific path and signals the overlay; env var keeps the public repo clean.
No-code separate-repo workflow — rejected by user: loses graph/MCP union (sensitive notes would be files only).
